reproducible_artifact: Lineage-check section in the deprecation runbook (shared with 2.26 but with the paired-replacement axis named explicitly): every deprecation must name both predecessor and successor systems before approval.
embodiment_sketch: Extend the deprecation runbook's 'Lineage Check' section with required fields: (1) System being deprecated; (2) System(s) it replaced (with approximate replacement date); (3) Successor system (currently planned); (4) Anticipated next-successor (architectural direction). If both directions of the lineage are populable, the deprecation is operational and ships on schedule. If only the predecessor is known, escalate — the absence of a successor suggests capability is being dropped rather than replaced.
verdict_reason: The artifact is concrete and copyable. The verse's paired-symmetric claim (born ↔ dies, dies ↔ reborn) is encoded as the bidirectional lineage requirement. A senior engineer could add this Monday.
